title: Carson City, NV Area Tourist Attractions

attractions.name: Battle Born Hall in the Nevada State Capitol Visit Carson City
attractions.category: Museum, Historic Site Visit Carson City, Travel Nevada
attractions.description: Battle Born Hall is a 2,000-square-foot museum located on the second floor of the Nevada State Capitol building that honors the characters and events that shaped the state's history. The exhibit, titled "Trailblazing Nevada," leads visitors through five thematic areas covering prehistory, the mining boom, statehood, and the 21st-century outlook. Visit Carson City, Travel Nevada

Housed in the historic Sweetland building, it offers a classic atmosphere where guests can enjoy premium handpicked cigars, craft drinks, and spirits. The establishment is known for its extensive whiskey collection and welcoming environment for socializing and unwinding (Capital City Saloon).

The park is a popular destination for hikers and birdwatchers, featuring trails that connect to the larger Prison Hill and Silver Saddle Ranch recreation areas. It is particularly known for its vibrant fall foliage and local owl populations. Visit Carson City, Gabriella Viola

attractions.name: Chollar Mine Tour
attractions.description: The Chollar Mine is a registered historic landmark and the site of one of the richest silver strikes in American history. Visitors can take a guided 400-foot underground walk to experience the authentic working conditions of Comstock Lode miners, seeing original silver ore veins and square-set timbering. Tripadvisor, Chollar Mine Tour

attractions.hours: Daily: 11:00 AM – 4:15 PM (Seasonal); Winter hours vary by weather (call for availability) Chollar Mine Tour, Chollar Mine Tour
attractions.days_open: Daily (Seasonal: May - October) Travel Nevada, Inked with Wanderlust
attractions.admission: Adults: $15.00, Children (5-12): $10.00, Children (under 5): Free, Paranormal/Night Tours: $125/hour (2-hour minimum for up to 4 guests, $30 per additional guest) Inked with Wanderlust, Chollar Mine Tour
highlights: ['400-foot guided walking tour into an authentic 1850s silver mine tunnel', 'Demonstrations of historic square-set timbering and pneumatic drills', "Viewing the famous 'blue clay' silver-rich ore veins in the mine walls", 'Extensive outdoor gallery of antique mining equipment, including ore carts and water wheels', "Experiencing 'miner's darkness' as guides demonstrate light from single candles", 'Small onsite museum featuring historic maps and artifacts from the Comstock Lode era']
attractions.tips: The mine maintains a consistent cool temperature, so a light jacket is recommended; wear sturdy, comfortable walking shoes as the tunnel floor can be wet and muddy. Note that this tour is cash-only and requires wearing provided hard hats due to low timbers. Inked with Wanderlust, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Comstock Gold Mill
attractions.description: The Comstock Gold Mill is the last fully operational stamp mill in the state of Nevada, providing a rare opportunity to see 19th-century mining technology in action. Built in 1864, the mill was originally used to process gold and silver rich ore from the Comstock Lode by breaking it down through a powerful stamping process. Today, visitors can experience the rumble of the restored 1860s-era machinery and learn about the history of ore recovery through narrated tours led by expert caretakers.
attractions.address: 435 F Street, Virginia City, NV 89440
attractions.hours: Thursday – Monday, 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Seasonal: May to October) Travel Nevada
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday (May through October) Travel Nevada
attractions.admission: Adults: $10, Children (12-18): $5, Kids (12 and under): Free Travel Nevada
highlights: 'The last fully operational stamp mill in Nevada [Travel Nevada', 'Original machinery built in 1864 by Joshua Hendy in San Francisco Visit Virginia City', 'Authentic demonstration of gold and silver ore processing and recovery Visit Virginia City', 'Restored historic mining equipment dating back to the Comstock Lode era Visit Reno Tahoe', "Narrated educational tours that bring Virginia City's mining history to life Travel Nevada"]
attractions.tips: The mill is only open seasonally from May to October, so plan your visit accordingly. While admission fees are low, the site depends heavily on donations for its continued preservation and operation. Large groups of up to 60 can be accommodated by appointment, and it is recommended to ask about school or tour group discounts when making reservations. Travel Nevada

attractions.name: Courthouse Slammer & County Museum
attractions.description: Housed within the state's oldest continuously operating courthouse, this museum is located inside a historic two-tiered jail cellblock. It features exhibits on the Comstock Lode, legendary gunslingers, and the diverse cultures that shaped the American West. Visitors can explore original jail cells while learning about the legal and social history of Storey County.
attractions.address: 26 South B Street, Virginia City, NV 89440
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free (Donations suggested)
highlights: ['Historic two-tiered jail cellblock repurposed into museum exhibits', 'Life-sized statue of Justice on the building facade, uniquely depicted without a blindfold', 'Exhibits on the Comstock Lode, Mark Twain, and the Territorial Enterprise', 'Stories of gunslingers, saloons, and the "Millionaires Club" within original jail cells', "A continuing video and slide show detailing the area's rich historical and current events", 'Display of a mask belonging to the famous bank robber John Dillinger']
attractions.tips: The museum is located inside an active courthouse, so visitors should be mindful of clerical offices and court proceedings. Donations are encouraged to support the preservation of exhibits. Parking may be available in the courthouse lot on weekends or late in the day, though the museum itself is only open during weekday business hours.

It features two distinct courses: the East Course, known for its user-friendly and walkable layout, and the West Course, which provides a challenging desert links-style experience for seasoned players. Managed by Duncan Golf, the facility also includes a driving range, a pro shop, and an on-site bar and grill (Eagle Valley Official Site).

attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, Brewery (Tripadvisor, Carson City Chamber)
attractions.description: Great Basin Brewing Co. is Nevada's most award-winning brewery, located in the heart of Carson City. This vibrant brewpub serves a selection of 15 award-winning craft beers on tap alongside hearty pub fare like wood-fired pizzas, burgers, and their famous Ichthyosaur "Icky" IPA bread. It offers a family-friendly atmosphere with a pet-friendly outdoor patio for guests to enjoy during warmer months (Great Basin Brewing Co.).

attractions.name: Historic C Street (Virginia City Historic District)
attractions.description: Historic C Street is the mile-long main thoroughfare of Virginia City, a National Historic Landmark District that preserves the 19th-century mining heritage of the Comstock Lode. The street features authentic Victorian-era architecture, wooden boardwalks, and a variety of saloons, shops, and museums that transport visitors back to the Old West. Travel Nevada, American Planning Association

attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Visitor Center to pick up maps and discounted attraction passes; wear comfortable shoes for the uneven boardwalks; many attractions have seasonal hours (best visited May–October). Visit Virginia City, Travel Nevada

attractions.name: Historic Delta Saloon
attractions.description: The Historic Delta Saloon is a legendary landmark in Virginia City that has been a central gathering place since the 1860s. Famous for its rich Wild West heritage, it was once a favorite haunt of Mark Twain and remains home to the notorious 'Suicide Table.' Today, it offers a blend of historic charm, gaming, and dining in the heart of the Comstock Lode. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 18 S C St, Virginia City, NV 89440
attractions.hours: Mon - Tues 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Wed - Sun 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: "Home to the infamous 'Suicide Table,' a 19th-century faro table with a legendary past ([Official Website)", 'Frequent haunt of author Mark Twain during his time as a reporter in Virginia City (Official Website)', "Original historic bar and interior dating back to the town's mining boom era (Visit Virginia City)", 'Features a newly renovated casino floor with modern slot machines (Airial Travel)', 'Includes the Miners Table restaurant (formerly Sawdust Steakhouse) serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visit the 'Suicide Table' to read about its eerie history and take photos of the authentic 19th-century decor. The saloon is generally accessible, though be mindful of the town's historic uneven wooden sidewalks. (Airial Travel)

attractions.name: Historic Fourth Ward School Museum and Archives
attractions.description: The Historic Fourth Ward School Museum is a majestic four-story, Second Empire-style wooden school building completed in 1876 in Virginia City, Nevada. It is the last remaining structure of its kind in the United States, originally built to accommodate over 1,000 students during the Comstock Lode mining boom and now serving as a cultural center and interpretive museum. Official Website, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Jack C. Davis Observatory
attractions.description: The Jack C. Davis Observatory is a research-level facility at Western Nevada College that offers the community unique opportunities to explore the cosmos. Situated on the eastern edge of the Sierra Nevada mountains, it features multiple interior and exterior telescopes and hosts regular Saturday night "Star Parties" with free lectures and stargazing. The facility also serves as a hub for astronomical research and includes a weather station and the educational Planetary Walkway. (Western Nevada College, Visit Carson City)
attractions.address: 2269 Vanpatten Ave, Carson City, NV 89703
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: Closed to public (Open for students/classes); Saturday: 6:30 PM – 11:30 PM (Doors open 6:30 PM, Lectures 7:00 PM); Sunday: Closed (Western Nevada College, Waze)
attractions.admission: Free (Western Nevada College, Carson Now)
highlights: ['Saturday Night Star Parties featuring public telescope viewing of the moon, stars, and planets.', 'The Planetary Walkway, an educational trail with sandstone sculptures representing solar system bodies.', 'Diverse telescope array including two reflecting telescopes and a specialized solar telescope.', 'Free weekly astronomy lectures led by professors and guest scientists.', 'On-site weather station providing real-time meteorological data for the region.', 'Research-grade equipment used by students and the local astronomical society.']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes before the scheduled lecture on Saturday nights to secure a seat, as these events are popular. Dress in layers because nighttime temperatures on the hill can be significantly cooler than in the city. Utilize the Planetary Walkway for a scenic and educational approach to the observatory from the main campus. (Visit Carson City, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Western Nevada College Campus, Silver Oak Golf Course, Nevada State Museum, Nevada State Capitol, Carson City Freeway Trail (Tripadvisor, Around Carson)

attractions.name: Kings Canyon Waterfall
attractions.description: Kings Canyon Waterfall is a scenic 25-foot cascading waterfall located just a five-minute drive west of downtown Carson City. The hike to the falls is a short 0.25-mile walk from the trailhead, making it one of the most accessible natural attractions in the region. While it remains a peaceful destination year-round, the waterfall is most impressive during the spring snowmelt. (Visit Carson City, Everything Carson)
attractions.address: 4450 Kings Canyon Rd, Carson City, NV 89703
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week (Travel Nevada, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Carson City, Travel Nevada)
highlights: 'Scenic 25-foot cascading waterfall ([Visit Carson City)', 'Extremely accessible 0.25-mile hike suitable for families (Everything Carson)', 'Panoramic views of Carson City, Washoe Lake, and the Virginia Range (Komoot)', 'Access to the extensive Ash-to-Kings Trail system (Carson City Government)', 'Abundant spring wildflowers and lush greenery (Komoot)', 'Recently renovated trailhead with paved parking and restrooms (U.S. Forest Service)']
attractions.tips: Dogs are strictly prohibited on the Lower Waterfall Trail to protect the city's drinking water watershed. The falls are best viewed in the spring when snowmelt is highest; summer visits can be hot due to limited shade. Wear sturdy footwear as the trail can be muddy or steep in sections. (Carson City Government, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Ash Canyon, Borda Ranch (historic marker), Washoe Lake, Nevada State Capitol, downtown Carson City (Carson City Government, Visit Carson City)

attractions.name: Kit Carson Trail
attractions.description: The Kit Carson Trail is a 2.5-mile self-guided path through Carson City's West Side Historic District. It features nearly 50 landmarks, including 1800s-era Victorian-style mansions, museums, and churches that tell the story of Nevada's capital city.
attractions.address: 600 N. Carson St. (Start of trail at Nevada State Museum)
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Daily)
attractions.admission: Free (Self-guided walking tour)

attractions.tips: Pick up a printed map at the Carson City Visitor Center or use the interactive audio tour on your smartphone. The trail is marked by a blue line and bronze medallions on the sidewalk.

attractions.name: Liberty Engine Company #1 State Firemen’s Museum (Comstock Firemen's Museum)
attractions.description: The State Firemen’s Museum, also known as the Comstock Firemen’s Museum, is a historic institution dedicated to preserving the rich heritage of firefighting in the Comstock region and throughout Nevada (Comstock Firemen's Museum). Housed in a building that dates back to the aftermath of the Great Fire of 1875, it features an extensive collection of antique firefighting apparatus, hand-drawn engines, and vintage gear (Travel Nevada). Visitors can explore a variety of artifacts, from 19th-century leather helmets to motorized vehicles that played a vital role in protecting the community (Comstock Firemen's Museum).
attractions.address: 125 South C Street, Virginia City, NV 89440
attractions.hours: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily during the season (Comstock Firemen's Museum)
attractions.days_open: Daily (May – October); Weekends (November – December, weather permitting) (Comstock Firemen's Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations are appreciated and encouraged) (Comstock Firemen's Museum)
highlights: "1839 Christian Hight four-wheel hand-drawn hose carriage, the oldest fire apparatus in Nevada ([Comstock Firemen's Museum)", "1856 Lysander Button & Co. hand-pumped fire engine used in various historic films (Comstock Firemen's Museum)", 'Antique leather fire helmets and vibrant red uniform shirts from the volunteer era (Travel Nevada)', "Extensive collection of vintage fire extinguishers and early 20th-century fire toys (Comstock Firemen's Museum)", 'Historic photographs and records detailing the brave actions of the Liberty Engine Company No. 1 (Travel Nevada)', "A collection of movie-star fire apparatus that have appeared in films like 'In Old Chicago' and 'The Harvey Girls' (Comstock Firemen's Museum)"]
attractions.tips: The museum is staffed by volunteers and relies on donations for its upkeep, so consider contributing what you can (Travel Nevada). It is a relatively small museum that can be explored in 30 to 60 minutes, making it a perfect quick stop while walking along C Street (Tripadvisor). Check the weather if visiting in late autumn, as weekend hours are subject to availability and conditions (Comstock Firemen's Museum).

attractions.name: Nevada Governor's Mansion
attractions.description: The Nevada Governor’s Mansion is the official residence of the Governor of Nevada and their family, located in the heart of Carson City. Completed in 1909 and designed by George A. Ferris, the Classical Revival-style mansion features elegant Georgian and Jeffersonian motifs, including fluted Ionic columns and a wraparound porch. It serves as a primary historical landmark and a centerpiece of the city's West Side Historic District. (Visit Carson City, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 606 Mountain St
attractions.hours: Private residence; interior access is generally restricted to Nevada Day (late October); grounds are visible daily (Airial Travel, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Grounds visible daily; Interior tours on Nevada Day only (Airial Travel, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel, Visit Carson City)
highlights: "Official residence of Nevada's governors since 1909 ([Visit Carson City)", 'Classical Revival architecture designed by George A. Ferris (Wikipedia)', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia)', 'Key stop along the historic Kit Carson Trail (Travel Nevada)', "Reputed to be one of Nevada's most haunted locations (Hauntworld)", 'Features a grand entry hall and formal dining room on the first floor (Visit Carson City)']
attractions.tips: As the mansion is a private residence, visitors should primarily enjoy the architecture from the sidewalk as part of the Kit Carson Trail walking tour. The only regular opportunity for an interior tour is during the Nevada Day open house in late October. (Tripadvisor, Visit Carson City)

attractions.name: Nevada State Capitol
attractions.description: The Nevada State Capitol is a historic sandstone building that has served as the seat of Nevada's government since 1871. It is the second-oldest capitol building west of the Mississippi River and currently houses the Governor's Office while also serving as a museum with exhibits on the state's path to statehood and its "Battle Born" identity. Visit Carson City, Wikipedia

attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Self-guided tours); Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Battle Born Hall) / 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Walk-in tours); Sunday: Closed Visit Carson City, Travel Nevada
attractions.admission: Free Visit Carson City, Travel Nevada

attractions.tips: Visit on a Saturday for first-come, first-served walk-in tours led by docents. Group tours should be scheduled at least two weeks in advance. ADA access is available through the main entrance on the east side. Visit Carson City

attractions.description: The Red Dog Saloon is a historic cornerstone of Virginia City, Nevada, famously recognized as the birthplace of San Francisco-style psychedelic rock and roll in the mid-1960s. Today, it operates as a family-friendly venue featuring a full bar and a popular pizza parlor, while continuing its legacy of hosting live music and open mic nights. Visitors can enjoy a rich historical atmosphere alongside fresh, handmade pizza and a variety of local entertainment (Travel Nevada, Red Dog Saloon History).

attractions.description: The Silver Terrace Cemeteries are a collection of historic burial grounds dramatically situated on the windswept hillsides of Virginia City. It serves as a profound monument to the diverse population of pioneers and miners who shaped the Comstock Lode. Visit Virginia City, Travel Nevada

attractions.description: St. Mary in the Mountains Catholic Church is a prominent historic landmark and Nevada's oldest Catholic church, originally established in 1860 and rebuilt after the Great Fire of 1875. Often called the 'Bonanza Church,' it features striking Gothic architecture with soaring spires, intricate stained glass windows, and a basement museum that showcases religious artifacts and Comstock mining history. The church remains an active parish while serving as a cultural center for visitors to explore the spiritual and pioneer heritage of Virginia City. Travel Nevada, Wikipedia

Visitors can explore exhibits ranging from mineral collections and blacksmith shops to Victorian-era costumes and household goods. Travel Nevada, Visit Virginia City

attractions.name: Ash Canyon (Ash Canyon Road & Trails) (Carson City Official Site)
attractions.category: Park / Open Space (Carson City Official Site)
attractions.description: Ash Canyon is a scenic recreational area and vital watershed located in the Carson Range immediately west of Carson City, Nevada. It features a diverse system of single-track trails and a seasonal road that provides the only motorized access to the Sierra Crest in the area, leading to the popular Hobart Reservoir. (Carson City Official Site)

attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Open 24/7 for trails; Road open seasonally) (Waze, Carson City Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Carson City Official Site)
highlights: 'Ash to Kings Trail: A 7-mile single-track trail offering panoramic views and connecting Ash and Kings Canyons ([Carson City Official Site)', 'Hobart Reservoir: A popular alpine fishery accessible via Ash Canyon Road and a short hike (Carson City Official Site)', 'Mountain Biking: Widely considered one of the best areas for mountain biking in Carson City with a variety of technical and flowy trails (MTB Project)', "Diverse Trail System: Includes named routes like Bob's Trail, Vicee Rim Trail, and the Creek Trail (Carson City Official Site)", 'Seasonal Road Access: Ash Canyon Road provides a rugged 4.5-mile drive into the high-elevation backcountry (Carson City Official Site)', 'Scenic Vistas: Dramatic views of Eagle Valley, Carson City, and the surrounding Sierra Nevada peaks (Carson City Official Site)']
attractions.tips: The area has no services, so visitors must pack in all water and supplies. Ash Canyon Road is a steep, narrow one-lane dirt road that requires high-clearance 4WD vehicles and is typically only open from Memorial Day through October. Hikers and bikers should prepare for little shade and be mindful of multi-use trail etiquette. (Carson City Official Site, Visit Carson City)

attractions.description: The Ash to Kings Trail is a premier 7-mile single-track trail that links Ash Canyon and Kings Canyon above the west side of Carson City. This popular non-motorized path offers stunning views of Eagle, Washoe, and Carson Valleys while winding through forested sections of Jeffrey pines and aspens (Visit Carson City, Forest Service).

attractions.name: Capital to Tahoe Trail (Cap to Tahoe)
attractions.description: The Capital to Tahoe Trail is a premier 9.8-mile single-track trail that provides the first non-motorized connection between Nevada's capital and the Lake Tahoe Basin. Completed in 2023, the trail traverses diverse terrain from high-desert hillsides to alpine pine forests, offering spectacular views of Washoe Valley and the surrounding mountains. It serves as a vital link to the 170-mile Tahoe Rim Trail, enabling long-distance journeys toward the Pacific Crest Trail and beyond (Visit Carson City, Singletracks).

attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset daily (Nevada State Parks)

attractions.admission: Free for trail access; $10-$15 per vehicle for parking at Spooner Lake State Park trailhead (Nevada State Parks)
attractions.phone: (775) 887-2262 (Carson City Parks), (775) 315-6763 (Muscle Powered) (Nevada Trail Finder, Partnership Carson City)
highlights: 'First single-track connection from Carson City to the Lake Tahoe Basin and the world ([Visit Carson City).', 'Connects directly to the 170-mile Tahoe Rim Trail, providing access to the Pacific Crest Trail (Sierra Rec Magazine).', 'Sweeping panoramic views of Carson City, Washoe Valley, and the Sierra Nevada range (The Nevada Independent).', 'Climbs to a peak elevation of 8,382 feet through diverse ecological zones (Kathryn Reed).', 'Awarded the 2024 Achievement Award for Community Linkage by the Coalition for Recreational Trails (Singletracks).', 'Collaborative project involving Muscle Powered, Nevada State Parks, the U.S. Forest Service, and private landowners (Visit Carson City).']
attractions.tips: Carry the '10 essentials' as the trail is remote with little to no cell service and rapid weather changes. Mountain bikers often prefer a point-to-point shuttle from Tahoe down to Carson City for a mostly downhill experience. Be alert for multi-use traffic including hikers, bikers, and equestrians (Visit Carson City, Singletracks).

The site offers a glimpse into the lives of one of western Nevada's most influential ranching families and the founding of the town of Minden.

It is famously known as the only golf course located directly on the shores of Lake Tahoe, offering breathtaking views of the water and surrounding snow-capped mountains. The course has a rich history of hosting prestigious events, including the U.S. Senior Open and the annual American Century Championship. Official Website, Wikipedia

It comprises multiple units including Sand Harbor, Spooner Lake, and Cave Rock, offering diverse natural resources from high alpine mountains to crystal-clear lake waters (Wikipedia).

attractions.hours: Daily: 8:00 AM to 1 hour after sunset; Visitor Center: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Tues-Fri) (Official Website)

attractions.tips: Day-use reservations are required for vehicle entry from May 1 through September 30 between 8:00 AM and 10:30 AM. Arrive early as parking lots fill quickly, especially during summer months. attractions.name: Tahoe Rim Trail (TRT)
attractions.description: The Tahoe Rim Trail is a premier 165-mile long-distance loop trail that circumnavigates the ridgelines surrounding Lake Tahoe. Passing through two states and six counties, it offers spectacular alpine vistas, winds through thick forests and open meadows, and reaches elevations of over 10,000 feet.
attractions.address: 128 Market St. Ste. 3E, Stateline, NV 89449 (Association Office); 1536 South Carson St, Carson City, NV 89701 (Carson Ranger District)
attractions.hours: Trail: Open 24 hours; Office: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Day Use); Overnight permits required for Desolation Wilderness ($6/person/night + $10 reservation fee); Thru-hiking permits available through the TRTA
highlights: '165-mile loop around Lake Tahoe ([Wikipedia)', 'Highest point: Relay Peak at 10,338 feet (Wikipedia)', 'Includes a 50-mile overlap with the Pacific Crest Trail (Tahoe Rim Trail Association)', 'Passes through Desolation, Mount Rose, and Granite Chief Wilderness areas (Wikipedia)', 'Christopher’s Loop offers the "most photographed spot" on the trail (Tahoe Rim Trail Association)']
attractions.tips: Obtain permits early for overnight stays in Desolation Wilderness. Check current trail conditions and water availability on the official website before departing, as some sections are dry. Prepare for high-altitude weather and follow Leave No Trace principles.

attractions.name: Washoe Lake State Park
attractions.description: Nestled in a scenic valley between Reno and Carson City, Washoe Lake State Park offers breathtaking views of the Sierra Nevada, Carson, and Virginia mountain ranges. It is a premier destination for hiking, camping, and water sports, while also serving as a vital habitat for diverse wildlife including bald eagles, pelicans, and feral horses. Nevada State Parks, Travel Nevada
attractions.address: 4855 Eastlake Blvd., Carson City, NV 89704
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 365 days a year Travel Nevada
attractions.admission: Day-use: $5.00 ($10.00 for Non-NV vehicles); Boat launch: $10.00 ($15.00 for Non-NV vehicles); Camping: $15.00 per night ($20.00 for Non-NV vehicles) + $10.00 for utility hookups; Bike-in: $2.00 per bike Nevada State Parks
highlights: ['Stunning panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada, Carson, and Virginia mountain ranges.', 'Extensive trail network with over 35 miles for hiking, mountain biking, and equestrian use.', 'Diverse birdwatching opportunities featuring bald eagles, American white pelicans, and migratory waterfowl.', 'Dedicated equestrian facilities including an arena, corrals, and specialized camping sites.', 'Popular watersports hub for kayaking, windsurfing, and fishing for catfish and bass.', 'Unique 18-hole disc golf course weaving through sagebrush and seasonal wetlands.']
